purpose: Classify historical reuse-first scan findings into explicit migration, projection-boundary, and owner-decision worklists without weakening the strict diff no-resurrection gate.
machine_boundary: This surface classifies historical full-scan findings only. It does not waive new diff findings, prove risk elimination, prove release readiness, prove production readiness, prove domain readiness, prove owner acceptance, or authorize physical deletion.
false_ready_guard: worklist decision is not risk eliminated
worklist decision is not release ready
worklist decision is not production ready
worklist decision is not domain ready
worklist decision is not owner acceptance

active_src_already_zero.meaning: No current full-scan hit is assigned to active src/modules runtime queue migration; remaining queue hits are projection, contract, or fixture vocabulary.
No current full-scan hit is assigned to active src/modules update/package migration; remaining update/package hits are projection, contract, or fixture vocabulary.
No current full-scan hit is assigned to active src/modules observability migration; remaining observability hits are projection, command, contract, test, or catalog vocabulary.

allowed_projection_scope: tests and fixtures that assert queue lifecycle projection or historical vocabulary
contracts that preserve provenance, schema, or no-active-runtime-claim language
residual_scan_classification.false_completion_guard: The 147 residual queue findings are allowed projection vocabulary only; Temporal durable lifecycle is still incomplete until external Temporal workflow/history, managed worker, real executor closeout, and owner/domain refs are observed.

allowed_projection_scope: contracts that preserve update, rollback, manifest, digest, or package provenance vocabulary
tests and fixtures that assert owner-routed update/package boundaries
residual_scan_classification.false_completion_guard: The 93 residual update/package findings are allowed projection vocabulary only; App release owner, runtime substrate materializer, and capability package channel still need owner receipt or typed blocker evidence before any release/currentness claim.

allowed_projection_scope: contracts and tests that preserve refs-only evidence, receipt, attempt, runtime, or drilldown vocabulary
residual_scan_classification.false_completion_guard: The 455 residual observability findings are allowed projection vocabulary only; production observability remains incomplete until OTLP/exporter/live endpoint owner evidence and external collector consumption are observed.

required_evidence: external Temporal server workflow history/query readback
real executor closeout without ephemeral fixture substitution
owner receipt, typed blocker, human gate, or route-back refs tied to a domain/stage attempt
owner_route_worklist.stop_condition: Temporal lifecycle can be reconstructed from Temporal history plus OPL authority events, or the owner emits a typed blocker naming the missing external substrate.

owner_route_worklist.owner: OPL Managed Update / One Person Lab App release owner
required_evidence: App release owner receipt or typed blocker
managed update receipt projection consumed by the App read model
owner_route_worklist.stop_condition: App/release owner accepts, blocks, or routes the update package boundary through its release authority surface.

required_evidence: content-addressed descriptor or OCI-style digest readback
owner_route_worklist.stop_condition: Capability package lifecycle is routed through descriptor, digest, lock, and owner receipt surfaces, or the package owner emits a typed blocker.

required_evidence: OTLP or OpenTelemetry SDK exporter owner lane
live endpoint readback outside bounded smoke-only path
semantic convention mapping for trace, metric, log, and event refs
owner_route_worklist.stop_condition: Operator drilldown can read the same current owner delta through trace, metric, log, and event vocabulary from a live exporter/collector path, or Observability emits a typed blocker.

required_evidence: tombstone or provenance ref when deleting a private tail
owner acceptance, owner decision, or typed blocker
owner_route_worklist.stop_condition: Each domain owner either accepts the hosted/generated replacement boundary, emits a typed blocker, or explicitly preserves the tail with owner rationale.

completion_readback_rules: Full scan historical findings may be decisioned while owner-route work remains open.

allowed_projection_boundary means the matched line may remain as projection, fixture, contract, command, or catalog vocabulary; it does not mean the target runtime/update/observability capability is complete.

phase finding_count=0 means no current scanner hit is assigned to that active src migration item; it does not prove external live evidence, owner acceptance, release readiness, production readiness, or domain readiness.
A future closeout must report both reuse-first scan historical_decision_summary and this owner_route_worklist; either open owner_live_evidence_required item keeps the plan partial.

missing_evidence: external Temporal server workflow history/query readback
real Codex executor closeout without ephemeral fixture substitution
owner/domain refs tying Temporal history to the domain/stage attempt
items.legal_next_command_or_owner: Run `OPL_TEMPORAL_ADDRESS=<external-temporal> ./bin/opl family-runtime residency proof --provider temporal --live --json` from an owner-provided external Temporal environment, then route the readback to OPL Runway Runtime owner for receipt, typed blocker, or route-back.
items.stop_condition: Temporal lifecycle can be reconstructed from external Temporal history plus OPL authority events, or OPL Runway Runtime emits a typed blocker naming the missing external substrate.

items.owner: OPL Managed Update / One Person Lab App release owner
missing_evidence: App release owner receipt or typed blocker
managed update receipt projection consumed by the App read model
items.legal_next_command_or_owner: Route the managed-update receipt projection to the One Person Lab App release owner; release/currentness proof must come from the App release authority, not from Framework scan output.
items.stop_condition: App/release owner accepts, blocks, or routes the update package boundary through its release authority surface.

missing_evidence: content-addressed descriptor or OCI-style digest readback
items.legal_next_command_or_owner: Route package lifecycle through Pack/Connect owner surfaces with descriptor, digest, lock and materializer readback; do not promote Framework registry projection into package authority.
forbidden_completion_evidence: Connect registry projection without owner receipt
items.stop_condition: Capability package lifecycle is routed through descriptor, digest, lock, and owner receipt surfaces, or the package owner emits a typed blocker.

missing_evidence: OTLP or OpenTelemetry SDK exporter owner lane
live endpoint readback outside bounded smoke-only path
semantic convention mapping for trace, metric, log, and event refs
items.legal_next_command_or_owner: Open an OPL Observability owner lane for OTLP/OpenTelemetry SDK exporter and external collector consumption; bounded OpenMetrics smoke remains diagnostic only.
items.stop_condition: Operator drilldown can read the same current owner delta through trace, metric, log, and event vocabulary from a live exporter/collector path, or Observability emits a typed blocker.

missing_evidence: tombstone or provenance ref when deleting a private tail
owner acceptance, owner decision, or typed blocker
items.legal_next_command_or_owner: Route each private tail through its domain owner with no-active-caller readback and replacement OPL primitive refs before physical delete or acceptance claims.
items.stop_condition: Each domain owner either accepts the hosted/generated replacement boundary, emits a typed blocker, or explicitly preserves the tail with owner rationale.

completion_readback_rules: This preflight records missing live/owner evidence; it is not an owner receipt.
Every item remains open until owner receipt, live evidence, typed blocker, human gate, or route-back evidence is observed.
Framework scan, contract, docs, tests, and strict diff evidence cannot close these owner-live-evidence items by themselves.

false_ready_guard: accepted migration worklist means the historical finding still exists until migrated
central helper classification permits the shared boundary implementation only; module-local validators remain migration worklist items
update command registry projection does not prove managed update split complete, App release ready, production ready, or owner acceptance
diagnostic drilldown command projection does not prove OpenTelemetry collector/exporter consumption or production observability complete
test lane file-name classification does not waive production observability or operator drilldown migration findings
test fixture classification does not waive production migration findings
